data.title: State Street Q4 earnings beat on NII, inflows; issues 2026 guidance
data.description:
State Street (STT) shares dipped 3.1% in Friday morning trading after posting above-consensus guidance for expenses, although Q4 2025 earnings and revenue each came in better than expected.

The custodial bank sees 2026 expenses rising 3%-4% Y/Y, compared with the +1.6% Visible Alpha estimate. Also for the full year, it sees net interest income growing in the low-single-digits from a year before, vs. +3.8% Visible Alpha consensus, and fee revenue rising 4%-6%.

Q4 adjusted EPS of $2.97, exceeding the $2.84 average analyst estimate, rose from $2.78 in Q3 and $2.60 a year ago.

Q4 revenue of $3.67B, also beating the $3.62B expected, advanced from $3.55B in the prior quarter and $3.41B in Q4 2024.

Net interest income increased 12% sequentially and 7% Y/Y to $802M, surpassing the $765M Visible Alpha consensus.

In Q4, said Chairman and CEO Ron O'Hanley, "we achieved record fee and total revenue, reflecting consistent year-over-year growth, with continued strong performance across Investment Services, Investment Management, and our Markets franchise."

Total net inflows of $85B climbed from $26B in Q3 and $64B in Q4 2024.

Investment Servicing assets under custody or administration rose 4% Q/Q and 16% Y/Y to $53.8T. The annual gain was mainly driven by market appreciation and flows, State Street said.

Total expenses were $2.74B, up from $2.43B in Q3 and $2.44B in the year-earlier period.

Provision for credit losses slipped to $8M from $9M in Q3 and $12M in 2024's Q4.

Conference call at 11:00 a.m. ET.

data.title: Sinclair says Scripps refuses to engage on takeover attempt
data.description:
Sinclair Inc. (SBGI) said E.W. Scripps (SSP)  continues to rebuff its takeover attempts.

“Over the last few weeks, Sinclair has continued to reinforce to Scripps its willingness to engage on a proposed Sinclair-Scripps combination," Sinclair said in a statement  on Friday. "Scripps has refused the invitations to speak with its single largest shareholder and instead has stated its preference to execute its standalone plan."

The latest update comes after Scripps (SSP) last month rejected Sinclair's $7 a share cash and stock offer.  Semafor reported last month that Sinclair (SBGI) may start a fight for board seats this year after its offer was rejected. Sinclair first disclosed in November that it had taken a more than 8% stake in Scripps.

Sinclair (SBGI) also said on Friday that the previously announced strategic review of its broadcast business and work related to the separation of Ventures will continue.

Shares of Scripps fell 1.5%, while Sinclair edged higher by 0.4%.

data.title: Paychex announces new up to $1B stock buyback program
data.description:
• Paychex (PAYX) on Friday announced an up to $1B share buyback program.
  • The company said the new plan replaces its 2024 authorization to repurchase $400M of common stock.
  • In fiscal 2025, Paychex returned over $1.5B to shareholders through cash dividends and share repurchases.
  • Shares were +0.04% pre-market to $110.56.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Canaan receives Nasdaq notice for minimum bid price deficiency
data.description:
• Canaan (CAN) received a Nasdaq notice on January 14, 2026, for non-compliance with the $1 minimum bid price requirement after its ADSs traded below that level for 30 consecutive business days.
  • The company has been granted a 180-day compliance period, until July 13, 2026, to regain compliance.
  • Canaan stated it will monitor its share price and take reasonable measures, including a potential reverse stock split if necessary, to regain compliance.

  • See PAYX Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Ondas trades higher after raising revenue estimate
data.description:
Ondas Holdings (ONDS) was trading higher after the autonomous systems and private wireless solutions provider estimated Q4 2025 and full-year 2025 revenue above consensus.

Shares were +5.54% Friday pre-market to $13.53.

The West Palm Beach-based company expects Q4 revenue in the range of $27.0M-$29.0M, 51% higher year-over-year and against the $17.51M consensus.

For the full year ended December 31, 2025, the company expects revenue to be between $47.6M and $49.6M, up 23% from the prior year and against the $38.13M consensus.

In November 2025, Ondas increased its 2025 revenue target to at least $36M from $25M, and established a preliminary revenue target of at least $110M for 2026.

Ondas said it has upped its 2026 revenue target to a range of $170M to $180M, 25% higher than the previous company target of $140M. The 2026 revenue consensus stands at $140.62M.

The company is scheduled to hold its OAS Investor Day today, January 16, at 10:00 AM ET.

data.title: Cloudflare rebounds as investors digest deal for Human Native
data.description:
Cloudflare (NET) shares rebounded on Friday, rising more than 1% in premarket trading, as investors digested the announcement of its intent to acquire Human Native.

On Thursday, shares of the internet infrastructure company fell more than 2% after it said it would acquire Human Native, which bills itself as an artificial intelligence data marketplace. The deal would further expand Cloudflare into the realm of transactions between AI developers and content creators, the company said in a statement.

“Content creators deserve full control over their work, whether they want to write for humans or optimize for AI,” Cloudflare CEO and co-founder Matthew Prince said in the release. “Last year we gave publishers and creators the tools to control which bots can access their content, but the real goal has always been to help create a new economic model that actually works for the next phase of the Internet. The Human Native team will help us accelerate the next phase, where we build the tools that allow content to be discovered, priced, and purchased through transparent channels. This acquisition is about building the tools needed to protect the longevity of the open Internet.”

Human Native was founded in 2024 and is backed by some of London's top venture capitalists. It has worked with companies in the tech and media spaces, including Google's (GOOG) (GOOGL) DeepMind, Figma (FIG), Bloomberg and others.

“We started Human Native with the goal of getting Generative AI out of its Napster era. We believe that creators should have control, compensation and credit when their work is used to power AI products, ensuring equitable compensation for rights holders while enabling responsible AI development,” Dr. James Smith, co-founder & CEO of Human Native, added. “Our discussions with Cloudflare, from Matthew to the entire team, have shown us that our values could not be more aligned, we believe in the same mission and together we will have the opportunity to deliver solutions at Internet scale.”

Financial terms of the deal were not disclosed, and Cloudflare did not say when the acquisition would close.

Separately, on Friday, Cloudflare announced it would be acquiring The Astro Technology Company team, the creators of the Astro web framework. Terms of the deal were not disclosed.

Investment firm Citi also cut its price target on Cloudflare to $260 from $318 on Friday, but said shares were at a “compelling entry point” after the recent pullback.

(This story has been updated to include additional merger news and analyst commentary.)

data.title: HP and Logitech get rating cuts, while Garmin upgraded at Barclays
data.description:
Barclays downgraded ratings of HP (HPQ) and Logitech International (LOGI), while upgrading Garmin (GRMN).

HP (HPQ)

The firm downgraded HP to Underweight from Equal weight and lowered the price target to $18 from $24.

The analysts believe secular challenges across both personal computers, or PCs, and Printing, combined with a lack of catalyst, will cause the stock to remain pressured in 2026. The analysts also expect the current memory cycle to pressure HP's PC business with further downside potential in the year.

The analysts said that although memory cycles are nothing new for HP, the degree and intensity of the current cycle could pressure units materially worse than prior cycles. Though near-term the business could see a pull-in bump from customers looking to secure now with known pricing versus later in the year, the analysts expect demand could degrade.

Barclays noted that the commercial business should be more insulated, though it could see pushouts. HP has enough memory inventory on hand for the first half with the second half more of an issue on both supply and pricing, according to the analysts.

In addition, Barclays said that the Windows 11 refresh is largely done in the U.S. with the refresh now 60% captured, and it does not see much upside in the year. The analysts added that they expect there could be further margin degradation on both the gross and operating lines, given memory supply and competitive pressures.

Shares of HP fell about 2% premarket on Friday.

Garmin (GRMN)

Barclays upgraded Garmin to Equal weight from Underweight and raised the price target to $217 from $208. 

The analysts said that they are more positive on  Garmin given the undemanding valuation, diversification of the business, and continued strength in the wearables business (about 60% of total) that continues to benefit from positive secular trends around wellness.

The analysts view the company as more insulated from potential consumer contraction, given the more high-end, niche applications and hardware Garmin touts versus broader-user focused wearables.

Logitech International (LOGI)

Barclays firm cut Logitech's rating to Equal weight from Overweight and reduced the price target on the stock to $98 from $134.

The analysts said they are concerned about potential macro weakness with softening in the North America (almost 50% of total revenues) consumer market called out during the last earnings (largely gaming).

The analysts added that business-to-business, or B2B, remains less than 50% of the business, with about 60% of  Logitech's business B2C. Inflationary pressures and uneven macro could weigh on upside this year, though the analysts acknowledge the company’s focus on operational leverage.

Shares of Logitech dipped about 1% premarket on Friday.

data.title: TransDigm to buy two aerospace parts companies for combined $2.2B
data.description:
TransDigm (TDG) said Friday it agreed to acquire Jet Parts Engineering and Victor Sierra Aviation Holdings from Vance Street Capital for a combined ~$2.2B in cash.

Jet Parts Engineering designs and manufactures aerospace aftermarket solutions, primarily proprietary OEM-alternative parts and repairs, and it serves commercial, regional and cargo airline customers, as well as maintenance, repair and overhaul providers; nearly all of JPE's revenue comes from the commercial aftermarket.

Victor Sierra Aviation designs, manufactures and distributes proprietary PMA and other aftermarket parts serving the commercial aerospace end market, primarily the general aviation and business aviation sectors; nearly all of VSA's revenue comes from the commercial aftermarket.

"The companies' highly engineered, proprietary OEM-alternative parts and services generate nearly 100% commercial aftermarket revenue," TransDigm (TDG) CEO Mike Lisman said; the two companies generated a combined ~$280M in revenues in 2025.
